revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= |名称=方県津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1 |位置= |祭神=丹生川上摩須郎女命 |社格= |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''方県津神社'''（かたがたつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[神社]]である。
[[美濃国]][[方県郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の方県津神社とされている。但し、美濃国方県郡の方県津神社に関しては他にも[[津神社]]、懸神社などの幾つかの論社がある。
本殿、拝殿などは[[古墳]]上にある。
== 祭神 == *丹生川上摩須郎女命 :[[丹波道主王|丹波道主命]]（[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]の皇子）の妻。[[日葉酢媛命]]（[[垂仁天皇]]の皇后）の母でもある。
**丹生川上摩須郎女命の義父が[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]（[[伊波乃西神社]]主神）、孫が[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]（[[伊奈波神社]]主神）。五十瓊敷入彦命の后は[[渟熨斗姫命]]（[[金神社 (岐阜市)|金神社]]主神）であり、その子は[[市隼雄命]]（[[橿森神社]]主神）である。このことから、[[美濃国]]に深い関わりがあると言える。
== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]三田洞線「長良八代公園前」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）10番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）5番のりばより「K50 長良八代公園前」「K51 三田洞団地」「K55 彦坂真生寺」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:かたかたつしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜市]]

{{神社 |画像=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|方県津神社]] |名称=方県津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1 |位置= |祭神=丹生川上摩須郎女命 |社格= |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}

== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]三田洞線「長良八代公園前」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）10番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）5番のりばより「K50 長良八代公園前」「K51 三田洞団地」「K55 彦坂真生寺」行き

== 祭神 == *丹生川上摩須郎女命 :[[丹波道主王|丹波道主命]]（[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]の皇子）の妻。[[日葉酢媛命]]（[[垂仁天皇]]の皇后）の母でもある。
**丹生川上摩須郎女命の義父が[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]（[[伊波乃西神社]]主神）、孫が[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]（[[伊奈波神社]]主神）。五十瓊敷入彦命の后は[[渟熨斗姫命]]（[[金神社 (岐阜市)|金神社]]主神）であり、その子は[[市隼雄命]]（[[橿森神社]]主神）である。このことから、[[美濃国]]に深い関わりがあると言える。
== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]三田洞線「長良八代公園前」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）10番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）5番のりばより「K50 長良八代公園前」行き
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたかたつしんしや}} [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]]

== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]三田洞線「長良八代公園前」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）10番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）5番のりばより「K50 長良八代公園前」行き

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine |Image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |Name=Katagatatsu Shrine |Location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |Coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |Deities=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |Shrine Rank=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |Founded=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Aga Shrine (Gifu City)|ja|縣神社}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kanakamu Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== Location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katagatatsu Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Mino Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]]

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |Deities=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katagatatsu Shrine}} [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Hōgō Tsu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Mino Province]] [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Hōgō Tsu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Gifu City]] [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Hōgō Tsu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Hōgō Tsu Shrine]][[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:方県津神社]]

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinai-sha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwase Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwase Shrine}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto : The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Itsuninomikoto|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}. The consort of Itsuninomikoto is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2023-11}} {{特筆性|date=2023-11}}{{神社 |画像=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|方県津神社]] |名称=方県津神社 |所在地=岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=丹生川上摩須郎女命 |社格=式内社（小）、郷社 |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''方県津神社'''（かたがたつじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]にある[[神社]]である。
[[美濃国]][[方県郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の方県津神社とされている。但し、美濃国方県郡の方県津神社に関しては他にも[[津神社]]、[[縣神社 (岐阜市)|縣神社]]などの幾つかの論社がある。本殿、拝殿などは[[古墳]]上にある。 == 祭神 == *丹生川上摩須郎女命 :[[丹波道主王|丹波道主命]]（[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]の皇子）の妻。[[日葉酢媛命]]（[[垂仁天皇]]の皇后）の母でもある。
**丹生川上摩須郎女命の義父が[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]（[[伊波乃西神社]]主神）、孫が[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]（[[伊奈波神社]]主神）。五十瓊敷入彦命の后は[[渟熨斗姫命]]（[[金神社 (岐阜市)|金神社]]主神）であり、その子は[[市隼雄命]]（[[橿森神社]]主神）である。このことから、[[美濃国]]に深い関わりがあると言える。
== 所在地 == *岐阜県岐阜市八代3丁目13-1
== 交通機関 == *[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]三田洞線「長良八代公園前」バス停下車 **[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北口）10番のりば、[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）5番のりばより「K50 長良八代公園前」行き
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたかたつしんしや}} [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]]

[[美濃国]][[方県郡]][[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の方県津神社とされている。但し、美濃国方県郡の方県津神社に関しては他にも[[津神社]]、[[縣神社 (岐阜市)|縣神社]]などの幾つかの論社がある。本殿、拝殿などは[[古墳]]上にある。 == 祭神 == *丹生川上摩須郎女命 :[[丹波道主王|丹波道主命]]（[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]の皇子）の妻。[[日葉酢媛命]]（[[垂仁天皇]]の皇后）の母でもある。

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinaisha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Gifu City|ja|岐阜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Shrine|qq=}}. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|ja|金神社 (岐阜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogane Shrine|qq=}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|ja|橿森神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimori Shrine|qq=}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}
{{translated page|ja|方県津神社|version=98070367|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }}

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg|250px|Katagatatsu Shrine]] |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35|27|40.3|N|136|46|21.5|E|scale:30000}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinaisha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. **The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. '''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504365]] Wikidata name: Katagatatsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504365]] Wikidata name: Katagatatsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35.461194|N|136.772639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinaisha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798}} }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|qq=|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|qq=|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
