It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:方県津神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504365]] Wikidata name: Katagatatsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504365]] Wikidata name: Katagatatsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35.461194|N|136.772639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinaisha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Gifu|WD=Q45798}} }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).

== Enshrined Deities ==
* Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto

**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].

** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).

== Enshrined Deities ==
* Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto

**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].

** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

== location ==
* 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture

== Transportation ==
* Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop.
** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]]. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at [[:en:Kogane Shrine|Kogane Shrine]]. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of [[:en:Kashimori Shrine|Kashimori Shrine]]. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}}. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|en|Kogane Shrine|WD=Q3198277|lt=Kogane Shrine}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|en|Kashimori Shrine|WD=Q3193694|lt=Kashimori Shrine}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}}. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|en|Kogane Shrine|WD=Q3198277|lt=Kogane Shrine}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|en|Kashimori Shrine|WD=Q3193694|lt=Kashimori Shrine}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."
{{Authority control}}

**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}}. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|en|Kogane Shrine|WD=Q3198277|lt=Kogane Shrine}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|en|Kashimori Shrine|WD=Q3193694|lt=Kashimori Shrine}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.

It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504366}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

{{Citation needed|date=November 2023}} {{Notability|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katagatatsu Jinja2008-1.jpg |name=Katagatatsu Shrine |location=3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture |coordinates={{Coord|35.461194|N|136.772639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto |type=Shikinaisha (small), Gō-sha |consecration_year=Unknown |Main Hall= |Festivals= |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} }}
'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11504366}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. However, there are other shrines identified as possible candidates, including {{ill|Tsu Shrine|ja|津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Agata Shrine (Gifu)|ja|縣神社_(岐阜市)|lt=Agata Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata Shrine (Gifu)|1=Agata Shrine (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11608141}}. The main shrine and worship hall are built on a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun).
== Enshrined Deities == * Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto
**The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. She is also the mother of {{ill|Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}, who became the empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}.
** Niukawakami Masurowake-no-Mikoto’s father-in-law is {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (enshrined at {{ill|Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}). Her grandson, {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=五十瓊敷入彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}, is enshrined at {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|qid=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine|1=Inaba Shrine}}. The consort of Prince Inishikiirihiko is {{ill|Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshihime-no-Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65272539}}, enshrined at {{ill|Kogane Shrine|en|Kogane Shrine|qid=Q3198277|lt=Kogane Shrine|1=Kogane Shrine}}. Their son is {{ill|Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja|市隼雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihayao-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who is the deity of {{ill|Kashimori Shrine|en|Kashimori Shrine|qid=Q3193694|lt=Kashimori Shrine|1=Kashimori Shrine}}. These familial connections signify a deep relationship with the Mino Province region.
== location == * 3-13-1 Yashiro, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture
== Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} Sanda-dō Line to the "Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae" bus stop. ** Board from the JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal (north exit, platform 10) or Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal (platform 5) heading for "K50 Nagara Yashiro Kōen-mae."

'''Katagatatsu Shrine''' (方県津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.
It is considered one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Katagata District|ja|方県郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagata District|1=Katagata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11504366}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. **The wife of {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
